preamble: The diphthong /ui/, a combination of a high back rounded vowel and a high front unrounded vowel, can undergo a phonetic transformation where its high front vowel component reduces and consonantizes into a voiced palatal approximant /j/. This process typically involves the loss or significant reduction of the initial vowel element of the diphthong.
phoneticEffects: Diphthong reduction, consonantization of the high front vowel component, loss of the initial vowel element

examples.note: The Ancient Greek diphthong /ui/ in words like 'huiós' (son) historically evolved into the voiced palatal approximant /j/ in Modern Greek 'γιος' [ˈʝos].
